sections.paragraph:
φιλάργυρος, ον (s. prec. two entries; Soph., X., Pla. et al.; Polyb. 9, 22, 8; 9, 25, 1; 9, 26, 11; Diod S 5, 27, 4; Epict.; Plut.; Cebes 34, 3; PPetr III, 53j, 14 [III b.c.]; 4 Macc 2:8; Philo; TestLevi 17:11) fond of money, avaricious Lk 16:14; 2 Ti 3:2; D 3:5. S. ἀφιλάργυρος.—M-M. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1056
